content_of_post_to_comment: My uncle, who I stupidly put on a pedestal, is giving me the silent treatment because I told him to please respect my wishes that I do not want to see my father. He may think he is punishing me, but I have already lived through this treatment. The only thing he is doing, is teaching me just how pervasive this patriarchy thing is. Older men not respecting their younger female relative’s right to make their own decisions and have their own boundaries is an epidemic. The only thing he is doing is making me not want to talk to him.
comment: There’s a difference between someone processing hurt and using silence as leverage. In families those two often get blurred, which makes boundary-setting hard to read in real time.
